name: Archaeology Society
summary: Our mission is to learn about, understand, and promote awareness of Archaeology and related fields such as Anthropology, Cultural Hertiage, and Art History all while having an awesome time! We offer an environment in which students can explore and disc...

name: Accounting Association
summary: Bridging the gap between the academic and professional worlds, the BU Accounting Association sponsors events that bring the industry to the classroom. Highlights include student internship panels, company information sessions, mock interviews...

name: Alpha Chi Sigma
summary: Alpha Chi Sigma is a national co-educational, professional chemistry fraternity dedicated to providing opportunities for study and leadership, as well as forming friendships and connections among students interested in chemistry and related fields.

name: Guerrilla Marketing Society
summary: Guerrilla Marketing Society (GMS) provides marketing consulting services to local businesses by offering them a marketing campaign plan for their business specifically using guerrilla marketing tactics. Our past partnerships include Expedia, Lady M, th...

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi is recognized as the premier developer of principled business leaders. As a professional business fraternity, Alpha Kappa Psi offers an inclusive community with opportunities for networking, mentorship, and professional development.

name: Black Business Student Association
summary: Boston University’s Black Business Student Association (BBSA) is an active resource on campus that bridges the opportunity gap for students of color through professional development and educational programming.

name: herNetwork - Boston University Women in Business
summary: herNetwork is an educational and empowering community for all genders and majors. With our motto, Women Mean Business, we aim to build a strong network that invests in ourselves and in one another. Please join us for our weekly events and conference!

name: Marine Science Association
summary: The Boston University Marine Science Association is a student group committed to learning about different marine science related topics by going on field trips, lectures, and exchanging information about personal marine-related experiences.

name: Undergraduate Psychology Association
summary: Students are encouraged to join the Undergraduate Psychology Association, an organization comprised of psychology majors, minors, and other interested students. The association is an independent, student-run organization that sponsors academic, career ...

name: American Cancer Society on Campus
summary: American Cancer Society On Campus (ACS On Campus) is a nationwide collaboration of students, faculty, and staff dedicated to eliminating cancer. We fundraise, celebrate, and support those who are affected, as well as advocate for awareness.

name: Undergraduate Women in Science and Engineering
summary: Undergraduate Women in Science and Engineering (uWISE) is an interdisciplinary, student-focused group providing community and a support network for all those who support women in the STEM fields through community outreach, mentoring, and social event

name: Marketing Club
summary: Boston University Marketing Club is committed to providing an opportunity for the BU community to explore all facets of marketing. Our members are driven to explore, diversify their knowledge, and grow within our four committees.

name: Tau Beta Pi Association
summary: Tau Beta Pi was founded in 1885 to mark in a fitting manner those who have conferred honor upon their alma mater by distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as undergraduates in the field of engineering, or by their attainments as alumni in th...

name: Stage Troupe
summary: Stage Troupe is Boston University’s oldest and largest extracurricular performing arts group for non-theatre majors. With 3 mainstage productions per semester, an annual summer show, and up to 10 special projects a year, there is involvement for anyone!

name: Callbacks
summary: The Callbacks are the only comedy group on campus that performs original sketch comedy, improvisational comedy, and produces video shorts. We are not just sketch. We are not just improv. We're just funny!

name: Mathematics Association at BU
summary: The Mathematics Association at BU aims to create a close-knit community among all students interested in mathematics, statistics, and mathematical sciences.

name: Mind and Brain Society
summary: MBS aims to inspire people of all academic disciplines to engage in conversations about humanity by using neuroscience as common ground. We host lectures, student-led discussions, social events, and volunteer opportunities throughout the year.

name: AdClub
summary: Explore the industry or expand your portfolio as a member. We are a place to meet creative peers and be inspired by their capabilities. As a client, get professional work with a dedicated team. Reach out to become a client or learn how to sign up.

name: Engineers Without Borders
summary: Engineers Without Borders - BU is a collegiate chapter directly affiliated with the national chapter of Engineers Without Borders USA. We work with communities around the world to design and implement sustainable engineering projects.

name: Umoja
summary: The purpose of Umoja is to create a healthy environment, for black students and all individuals who are interested in black culture, which actively united students, staff and alumni by providing pertinent educational, social, and community outreach pro...

name: Pre-Law Society
summary: The BU Pre-Law Society offers a myriad of events for undergraduates who are planning to apply to law school or may be interested in a legal career. Some of our events include hosting visiting law schools, LSAT practice exams/strategy sessions, and panels

name: Business & Technology Club
summary: The BU BizTech Club aims to target students who think critically, problem-solve, and strive to understand and utilize technology on an ongoing basis. We aim to educate and develop an understanding of career paths associated with a degree in MIS.

name: Pre-Law Review
summary: The Boston University Pre-Law Review is a student-run law journal, open to all Boston University undergraduates.  Founded in 1991, the Pre-Law Review has continued to foster awareness and provide commentary on major issues facing the law and the legal ...

name: Anime Club
summary: The Anime Club is a social group in which both anime enthusiasts and dabblers in the Boston University community can come watch, discuss, and learn more about anime. We provide weekly programming open to the BU community as well as group excursions.

name: Art History Association
summary: The Art History Association is an undergraduate student-run organization. We are open to anyone, regardless of your major or knowledge of art history. Throughout the year, we organize museum visits, networking, and social events.

name: Alianza Latina
summary: Alianza Latina is Boston University's largest Latinx student organization and the Latine Student Union. We are dedicated to providing students with programming that fosters community and encourages students to learn more about Latinx culture and identity

name: Stand Up Club
summary: BU Stand-up Club is an organization for stand-up comedy and those who love it. We aim to organize shows for campus comedians and invite professional comedians based on members' suggestions.

name: Public Relations Student Society of America
summary: The Public Relations Student Society of America (PRSSA) is the foremost organization for students interested in public relations and communications. We seek to advance the public relations profession by nurturing generations of future professionals.

name: Undergraduate Philosophy Association
summary: Boston University’s Undergraduate Philosophy Association holds weekly informal discussions on a variety of philosophical issues. In addition, we host lectures, resource meetings, group outings, underrepresented philosophy symposiums, and more.

name: Entrepreneur's Club
summary: BU Entrepreneurship Club is a place for all students of all backgrounds and all majors to become engaged with entrepreneurship. We focus on holding interesting events each semester that allow members or our club to meet fellow entrepreneurial students,.

name: Queer Activist Collective
summary: The Queer Activist Collective ("Q") is Boston University's largest LGBTQ+ student organization dedicated to ensuring the awareness, visibility, & full inclusion of the LGBTQ+ community.

name: Trans Listening Circle
summary: Trans Listening Circle (TLC) is a weekly-meeting club for people who are transgender, nonbinary, gender non-conforming, questioning, and more to find community on campus.

name: Fashion and Retail Association
summary: Fashion & Retail Association is a Boston University undergraduate organization, dedicated to connecting students to the competitive fashion and retail industry through all business functions, aspects of communication, and creative design.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers
summary:
SASE’s mission is to:

Prepare Asian heritage scientists and engineers for success in the global business world.
Celebrate diversity on campuses and in the workplace.
Provide opportunities for members to make contributions to their local communities.

name: Knitting Club
summary: This club is for those interested in the fiber arts particularly knitting and crochet.  BU Knitting club meets every Thursday from 7-8:30 pm in the GSU! Whether you knit or crochet, are a newbie or expert, feel free to stop by!

name: Outing Club
summary: The Outing Club strives to bring the joy of the great outdoors to the BU community. From urban explorations to trekking the White Mountains, we're 100% driven by the desire to take adventures beyond the BU campus. Check out linktr.ee/buoutingclub

name: Finance and Investment Club
summary: The BU Finance and Investment Club offers all students at Boston University an insight into the world of finance and an opportunity to learn by practical experience. The Club hosts interactive tutorials that explore topics to an extent above and beyond...

name: Hellenic Association
summary: The BUHA goal is to enlighten the Boston University campus and the greater Boston area with the language, art, culture, history, politics, beauty, and majesty of modern and classical Hellenism. We welcome and counsel all new hellenic students!

name: Society of Women Engineers
summary: The BU chapter of Society of Women Engineers (SWE) is a student section in National SWE. We're part of region F, the New England Region. Our mission is to empower women to achieve full potential in careers as engineers and leaders.

name: Rocket Propulsion Group
summary: BURPG is a primarily undergraduate student organization devoted to the testing and development of liquid rockets and rocket engines. We are open to all students who share a love of aerospace and learning.

name: Society of Professional Journalists
summary: The Boston University Society of Professional Journalists is a student chapter of the national organization, comprised of students within the BU journalism community. BU SPJ hosts educational events, provides a network between students and professional...

name: French Club
summary: The Organization aims firstly, to warmly welcome the French speaking community at Boston University, regardless of their level of fluency, to Boston and to the University and to facilitate their integration and provide them with support. Secondly, the ...

name: Association for Women in Mathematics: Student Chapter
summary: Association for Women in Mathematics (AWM) is a welcoming space for self-identified women interested in math, hosting social, academic, & professional math events and promoting equity in & access to the beautiful world of mathematics for ALL.

name: Data Science Association
summary: BUDSA is committed to providing students with data science tools and resources they need to make an impact in the ever-changing world of big data.

name: UNICEF Campus Initiative
summary: The UNICEF Campus Initiative is a nonprofit student-led organization that partners with the US Fund for UNICEF to educate, advocate, and fundraise for UNICEF.

name: American Studies Program Graduate Student Association (Graduate)
summary: The American Studies Graduate Student Association (AMSTU GSA) serves the American Studies and Preservation Studies graduate students through a variety of activities and opportunities.

name: Arts Administration Student Association
summary: The Boston University Arts Administration Student Association (AASA) is a Graduate group that was formed in the spring of 1995 to provide opportunities for social interaction, educational enrichment and career development for Arts Administration students

name: Graduate Chinese Students and Scholars Association - 47600
summary: BU Chinese Student Scholar Association (CSSA) was founded in 1994. It is a nonprofit organization of Chinese students and scholars who are currently studying or working at Boston University.

name: The DREAM Program
summary: DREAM is a non-profit mentoring program that pairs college students and children from affordable housing communities in Boston. If you're interested in becoming a DREAM mentor, please contact budreamrecruit@gmail.com!

name: Kappa Alpha Theta
summary: Kappa Alpha Theta exists to nurture each member throughout her college and alumnae experience. Now, more than ever, the Eta Chi chapter at Boston University feels the importance of sisterhood along with our strong commitment to scholarship and service.

name: Kidney Disease Screening and Awareness Program
summary: The Kidney Disease Screening and Awareness Program (KDSAP) is a student-run organization that provides free kidney health screenings and health education to underserved communities in Boston and in the Greater Boston Area. We provide programs geared to...

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta
summary: Alpha Epsilon Delta, the only national pre-health honor society, was founded in 1926 at the University of Alabama and is currently one of the 57 members of the Association of College Honor Societies. The primary objectives of AED include promoting the ...

name: Treblemakers
summary: The Boston University Treblemakers were formed in 1996 around three fundamental ideals: fun, love, and music. Sticking close to those values, the Treblemakers have become a tight-knit group with a large presence on Boston University's campus. Dozens of...

name: INK Illustration Club
summary: Are you interested in art, webtoons, writing, storyboarding, animating, or character design? Join INK!! We are a creative club and community of artists and writers from all backgrounds and mediums. We host workshops and events open to everyone!

name: CAS Student Government
summary:
The College of Arts & Sciences Student Government advocates for and represents the interests of the CAS student body. Our organization aims to host events, create initiatives, and foster an amazing family community for all in CAS! 

Insta: @cas_stugov

name: Delta Kappa Alpha
summary: Established March 16, 1936, Delta Kappa Alpha (DKA) is a National, Gender Inclusive, Professional Cinema Fraternity for cinematic arts students of character.

summary: The American Society of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology works to construct a national network of undergraduate students and faculty for the advancement of research, education, and science outreach. Our mission is to provide networking  and career de...

name: Aural Fixation
summary: BU's Treble-Voiced A Cappella Group

name: South Campus Hall Council
summary: South Campus Residence Hall Association is an organization built to direct and guide matters affecting student life in the South Campus residence halls in cooperation with the administration of the college and the apartment/residence hall councils. It ...

name: Psi Chi
summary: Psi Chi is the National Honor Society in Psychology, founded in 1929 for the purposes of encouraging, stimulating, and maintaining excellence in scholarship, and advancing the science of psychology. The Boston University Chapter of Psi Chi, the Interna...

name: Warren Towers Hall Council
summary: Section 1. Build a sense of community and hall identity and a residence that enhances the quality of life and addresses all students concerns.Section 2. Act as a liaison between hall staff and residents and to provide a channel for the expression of st...

name: Alzheimer's Buddies
summary: Alzheimer's Buddies is dedicated to connecting Boston University students with dementia residents in order to build friendships that reduce the social isolation experienced by this population.

name: Sargent College Honor Society
summary: Invitation to Juniors and Seniors in Sargent College who have achieved a GPA in the top 10% of their class. Induction is in the Fall each year. We focus on professional development and serve as a valuable connection to Sargent College alumni.

name: Theta Tau
summary: Theta Tau is a professional co-ed engineering fraternity founded at the University of Minnesota in 1904. It is the oldest, largest, and foremost fraternity for engineers that has grown to over 35,000 members and 91 chapters and colonies across the nati...

name: Phi Chi Theta Business Fraternity
summary: Phi Chi Theta Zeta Chapter is a professional co-ed business and technology fraternity for all majors and minors at Boston University. Originally established in the 1920s, the Zeta chapter was one of the eight original founding national chapters of PCT.

name: Saudi Cultural Club
summary: We unite Saudi and non-Saudi students through cultural events, celebrations, and career-building opportunities. By promoting belonging, understanding, and meaningful connections, we create a vibrant, inclusive community that celebrates Saudi heritage.

name: 180 Degrees Consulting
summary: 180 Degrees Consulting is the world’s largest student-run consultancy, dedicated to helping nonprofits and social enterprises maximize their impact. For more information on how to get involved, sign up for our mailing list.

name: Undergraduate World Languages & Literatures Association
summary: As a member of BUWA, you'll be a part of the diverse community that is World Literatures & Languages. We aim to bridge the gap between students spread out across the department, as well as facilitating communication between undergraduates and faculty.

name: Sisters United
summary: Sisters United strives to empower young Black and Brown women at Boston University through the provision of mentorship opportunities, on-campus engagements, and off-campus excursions.

name: Real Estate Club
summary: The Boston University Undergraduate Real Estate Club strives to educate the Questrom School of Business and greater Boston University undergraduate community about the real estate industry by creating opportunities for learning and professional network.

name: Lambda Chi Alpha
summary: Lambda Chi Alpha was founded here at Boston University by a law student named Warren A. Cole. We are the Alpha Chapter. As a fraternity we seek to offer the best fraternal experience, focusing on personal character development through leadership, schol..

name: Chemia
summary: Chemia is the undergraduate chemistry student organization at Boston University. We foster a community for students in the molecular sciences to support each other and host events for students to grow as a chemist. We aim to share our love for chemistry.

name: Latin American Student Association at Boston University
summary: LATAM is a welcoming space for people from all around Latin America. Our goal is to create a sense of community within BU, where Latin students can meet build friendships and network for future needs. We will debate, fundraise, and raise awareness.

name: Students for Reproductive Freedom
summary: BU Students for Reproductive Freedom advocates healthy/safer sexuality, sex positivity, abortion access, comprehensive sexuality education, and push for legislation that expands access to reproductive healthcare.  As a registered Planned Parenthood cam..

name: Sustainable Ocean Alliance
summary: The Sustainable Oceans Alliance is a youth-led organization that empowers students to become leaders in preserving the health and sustainability of our oceans.

name: Undergraduate Architecture Association
summary: The UAA is an organization for students interested in architecture, design, urbanism, and real estate. Our goal is to foster a community that will support students in furthering their study of the built environment through discussions, panels, and trips.

name: Chordially Yours
summary: Hi! We're Chordially Yours, a treble-voiced a cappella group founded in 2000 by Beth Heald ('03)! You can call us Chords for short. We believe in using music to empower and celebrate women and nonbinary people.

name: BosTones
summary: Each semester, the BU BosTones bring a unique soul and energy to the Boston a cappella scene. We are a co-ed group of undergraduates focused on delivering the hottest arrangements and a lethal sound.

name: Blockchain Club
summary: BU Blockchain Club invites all members within the Boston University community to engage themselves within the exciting and emerging market of blockchain, which is growing exceptionally and having more real world applications every single day.

name: Diversity in Law Association
summary: The BU Diversity in Law Association (DLA) strives to create a space where students, particularly minority students, can learn about how law can affect their lives and how they can contribute to society through a legal career. As members of this organiz...

name: Boston Political Review
summary: The Boston Political Review is a platform for undergrad and grad students to produce non-partisan work covering politics. The review's combination of academic and traditional articles has garnered the attention of thousands of viewers across the globe.

name: Beta Psi Omega
summary: We are a professional co-ed biology fraternity that provides members with leadership opportunities, fosters academic excellence, expands social networks, and generates a community of unique and driven students.

name: Board Games Club
summary: Board Games club is a catch-all organization for all your non-digital gaming needs. If it can be played on a table, someone in our club will be happy to play it with you!

name: Malaysian Cultural Association
summary: Malaysian Cultural Association has a two-pronged approach with community outreach, which is to act as a support system for Malaysian students at Boston University and to introduce and teach about Malaysian culture to the student community.

name: Upsilon Pi Epsilon
summary: Upsilon Pi Epsilon (UPE) at BU is an honor society dedicated to promoting excellence in technical and computing disciplines. UPE at BU will help prepare members for the industry through professional development, meaningful and interesting projects, and...

name: Epsilon Eta
summary: Epsilon Eta at Boston University is a multi-gender professional environmental fraternity that promotes a healthy and sustainable environment and connect students to industry, community service, and academia in pursuit of these goals.

name: CGS Student Government
summary: The College of General Studies Student Government Association (CGS SGA) is an organization which strives to represent the CGS student body and better connect them to the BU community through events, advocacy and communication.

name: Camp Kesem
summary: Kesem is a community, driven by passionate college student leaders, that supports children through and beyond their parent's cancer. A program of Kesem, Camp Kesem operates free summer camps for children who have been impacted by a parent's cancer.

name: School of Hospitality Administration Student Government
summary: The School of Hospitality Administration Student Government is a forum for the academic and social interests of the students of SHA. SHAgov coordinates programs and events within the school and represents SHA as part of the larger BU campus.

name: Association of Latino Professionals for America
summary: ALPFA is a national non-profit organization and the largest Latino Association for business professionals and students with 92K+ members nationwide, connections with hundreds of Fortune 1000 partners, and provides access to 50K paid summer internships.

name: Wheelock Student Government
summary: Undergraduate students are represented within the Wheelock College of Education and Human Development and Boston University by an active student government. In addition to planning and providing educational, social, and professional functions, . . .

name: Questrom Student Government
summary: Questrom Student Government's mission is to enhance the undergraduate student experience in the Questrom School of Business to ensure that we are creating an inclusive environment that enhances our undergraduate community and student life.

name: Terpsichore
summary: We are Terpsichore (terp-SIK-oh-ree), Boston University’s premier a cappella group! Affectionately known as Terps, we are a tight-knit, inclusive group of treble-voiced people dedicated to sharing our love of music.

name: Hack4Impact
summary: Background: Hack4Impact is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that was founded by University of Pennsylvania.Hack4Impact has many chapters at other universities, including Columbia University, Georgia Tech, and Boston University. The mission of Hack4Im...

name: Business Law Club
summary: The Boston University Business Law Club is organized to bring more awareness to the Law field. Through our classes, we have recognized a need for an organization that encourages people to challenge different ideas in a safe environment.

name: Music Business Club
summary: The Boston University Music Business Club serves as the hub of BU's music community, connecting anyone and everyone who wants to be involved in the music industry, including musicians, photographers, artist managers, PR professionals, and more.

name: Financial Technology Group
summary: FINTECH BU aims to introduce the Boston University community to the new and exciting field of financial technology and provide resources and opportunities for natural disruptors from all backgrounds.

name: Pride in Business
summary: Pride in Business is a premier business club dedicated to the support and professional development of LGBTQ+ business leaders of all majors.

name: Campus Activities Board
summary: This is a student-focused programming board for the Boston University campus. Focused on creating events and programs that will benefit the entire BU community, bringing students together in new ways, and planning fun events!

name: Girls Inc
summary: Girls Inc is an organization dedicated to empowering young girls and championing women's potentials and aspirations, to pursue broader futures through the mentorship of college-aged women.

name: Hearts for the Homeless Boston
summary: Hearts for the Homeless is an organization that provides free and informative blood pressure screenings and heart health education events to people experiencing homelessness in the Greater Boston Area.

name: CleanTech Club
summary: The BU CleanTech Club aims to create an engineering-focused community of students and faculty interested in the advancement of environmentally sustainable technology.

name: Art Club
summary: The aim of Boston University’s Art Club is to create a fostering artistic community where students can engage with one another through different forms of artistic expression.

name: Society of Physics Students
summary: Photon is a student-run organization that spreads the love of physics throughout the undergraduate community. We do field trips, demos, and more! You do not need to be a physics major to join - just interest and enthusiasm in learning about physics!

name: FORGE Design Studios
summary: FORGE Design Studios strives to prepare students for the growing and innovative field of design through workshops, professional development events, community outreach, and more. We aim to serve as a welcoming on-campus design resource and community.

name: Phi Alpha Delta
summary: Phi Alpha Delta’s Pre-Law Program assists undergraduate students in making an informed decision about pursuing a legal career, deciding which law school to attend, and preparing for the rigors of law school.

name: The Politica at Boston University
summary: The Politica is a global affairs organization with an online publication, podcast, and roundtable discussions. We believe that diversity and inclusivity are essential for the cultivation of insightful research and conversation.

name: The Alexander Hamilton Society
summary: AHS is a non-partisan, non-profit, national organization that seeks to identify, educate, and launch students into foreign policy and national security careers imbued with the Hamiltonian perspective of strong and principled leadership in global affairs.

name: Undergraduate Women in Economics
summary: UWE is a community of women and minority gender students who are pursuing a degree in Economics or are interested in the field. We host researchers and speakers and start conversations around what it means to be a minority student in Economics.

name: Women In Law
summary: WIL strives to fill the gap that exists for young women who are interested in a pre-law path. The mission of the club is to develop, nurture, & challenge students to pursue their law oriented dreams.

name: Study of Religions Club
summary: The Study of Religions Club (SOR) provides a social environment for BU students to casually engage in discussions about religion and traditions while connecting students with professional and academic resources in the Department of Religion.

name: Arab Student Organization
summary: BUArabs' mission lies in building cultural, academic, and industrial bridges between BU and the Arab world. We aim to create a platform to express our ideas and grow professionally, academically, and personally while celebrating our Arab heritage.

name: South Asian Student Association
summary: The South Asian Student Association is a cultural organization representing communities from Afghanistan, Pakistan, India, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh, the Maldives & Sri Lanka through a variety of professional, social and service events.

name: Moot Court Organization
summary: The Moot Court team's purpose is to educate undergraduate students about the American legal system, American jurisprudence, and the work of American attorneys, as well as to compete and aspire to win at the highest level of competition attainable.

name: Pre-Health Professionals Club
summary: PHPC is a Pre-Health Professional organization striving toward “helping people, together." Members dedicate their lives to the service of others philanthropically and through community service,  while always upholding academic excellence and integrity.

name: After School Newsletter
summary: BUAS is a newsletter club dedicated to encourage BU Chinese international students engage in local campus activities, explore Boston culture, and connect with other students through publishing newsletters, social media posts, videos, and events.

name: Barbell Club
summary: Boston University Barbell Club aims to become a reputable resource for fitness and weight training information as well as build a strong community and social outlet for training enthusiasts.

name: Society of Sci-Fi & Horror
summary: SOSH strives to foster a community for students who are enthusiastic about science fiction and horror motifs in books, movies, or any other art forms. We welcome “connoisseurs” and newcomers to the genres alike.

name: Alternative Investments Group
summary: BUAIG enhances the community's proficiency in non-public investments and develops well-rounded business professionals through a robust curriculum, a semester-long case competition, and professional development events.

name: Migration Tales
summary: Migration Tales is a multi-media platform that aims to amplify Migrant and first-generation American voices through storytelling, one story at a time.

name: The Central Asian Student Association
summary: CASA is the university-wide student organization tailored towards students from the Central Asian countries like Kyrgyzstan, Kazakhstan, Tajikistan, Uzbekistan, Turkmenistan and other Turkic countries.

name: Prison Outreach Initiative at BU
summary: BUPOI's mission is to serve and advocate for incarcerated individuals, both locally and throughout the US. We will do so by volunteering with and fundraising for incarcerated communities, as well as by educating fellow students about prison reform.

name: CDS Student Government
summary: The Computing and Data Sciences (CDS) student government serves the interdisciplinary student community at CDS. Through advocacy, communication, and collaboration with our fellow peers, we aspire to make CDS feel like a tight-knit community.

name: SHADES
summary: SHADES is a community-centered organization for Boston University’s queer and trans students of color.

name: Etc. Choir
summary: Etc. Choir is an inclusive, education-focused, member-centered choral ensemble that performs a wide range of music such as Western and Non-Western classical, musical theater, and popular styles of music in a choral setting.

name: Kappa Theta Pi
summary: Kappa Theta Pi is the nation's first technology-based professional fraternity, accepting students from all majors. KTP has five pillars: professional development, alumni connections, social growth, technological advancement, and academic support.

name: Arché, Boston University's Student-Led Journal of Philosophy
summary: Arché is a journal of philosophy edited & published by students of the CAS in coord. w/ the Dept. of Phil. We strive to highlight exceptional student work, providing some with the opportunity to publish their work for a general philosophical readership.

name: Sustainable Business Club
summary: The Sustainable Business Club is a place for students who care about doing well and doing good. We focus on businesses that can profit while also working to improve society. The club focuses on this crossroad and allows for experiences in this area.

name: Phi Gamma Nu
summary: PGN is a national business fraternity promoting diversity, equity, and entrepreneurial spirit, and integrity. Our goal is to foster professional development and provide a supportive community for young, motivated individuals preparing for future career.

name: Journalists of Color
summary: JOC is a student organization focused on career development, advocacy, and mentorship for journalists of color at BU and BU students interested in journalism. We intend on having bi-weekly meetings, special events, and lectures with guest speakers.

name: FilmBiz: Boston University’s Entertainment Business Club
summary: FilmBiz is Boston University’s first student organization dedicated to students interested in careers within the business and management areas of the entertainment industry (film, television, theater, music, etc).

name: The Film Production Club of Boston University
summary: The Boston University Short Film club is dedicated to educating passionate filmmakers and providing Boston University film students with an open, film-oriented community. We focus on producing short films, and hosting club events and outings.

name: Her Financial Future
summary: Her Financial Future focuses on teaching women about personal finance, investing, and becoming more financially independent. We host bi-weekly workshops on various topics, and are open to women & non-binary individuals of all majors & backgrounds! :)

name: Terrier Ride Engineering Club
summary: The purpose of this organization is to showcase the unique career field that exists within the theme park industry and create a community for theme park enthusiasts

name: Khmer Student Association at Boston University
summary: The Khmer Student Association at Boston University fosters a community of Khmer students, offers a space to explore Khmer culture, and helps Khmer Americans reconnect with their roots. Open to all BU students interested in Khmer culture.

name: Visual Journalists Association
summary: BUJVA is a professional club to support student photojournalism and other types of visual journalism.

name: Society For Biomaterials
summary: Boston University’s Student Chapter of the Society For Biomaterials promotes student research, education, and professional development in biomaterials and related disciplines while furthering the advancement of biomaterials research and education.

name: Venture Group BU
summary: We provide a professional and social learning environment to students who are passionate about Venture Capital or want to learn more about the industry. In our organization, we train sourcing analysts, host conferences, and engage in VC competitions.

name: Retail, Luxury & Consumer Association
summary: The Retail, Luxury & Consumer Association brings together students passionate about the luxury industry through speaker events, brand-partnered consulting programs, retail experiences, and an alumni mentorship program.

name: Latin American Business Association
summary: The Latin American Business Club (LABA) explores Latin America's role in global business. It is an organization, open to all majors, that connects students with industry leaders who have experience working in Latin America through events and workshops.

name: Survivor: Boston University
summary: Based on the reality TV show Survivor on CBS, Survivor BU emulates a “season” of the show in a semester-long social strategy game full of challenges and tribal councils, all while cultivating a wide and diverse community!

name: Interface of Science, Technology, Engineering, and Policy
summary: INSTEP is a student organization that bridges the gap between engineering, science, and public policy. Our mission is to empower students to explore how science and innovation interact with regulation, advocacy, and public impact.

name: The Pinky Toe Paper
summary: The Pinky Toe is a multimedia, intersectional-feminist satire publication that seeks to uplift marginalized students through laughter and creating a safe space to build community with other students are going through similar struggles and experiences.

name: American Civil Liberties Union at BU
summary: ACLU @ BU educates students on civil rights, civil liberties, and civic engagement through discussion, advocacy, and action. We foster understanding of legal, political, and social issues and empower students to become agents of change.

name: Women in Data Science
summary: Women in Data Science (WiDS) at BU is a student-led organization dedicated to empowering women and gender-diverse individuals in data science through workshops, networking, mentorship, and community events. All are welcome to join!

name: Untangle
summary: Untangle is a multimedia artist collective that aims to explore the sociopolitical experiences of Asian American communities by creating and publishing a print magazine and hosting community events that encourage learning, reflection, and dialogue.

name: LatinasBU
summary: LatinasBU empowers Latina and femme-identifying students at BU by building community, celebrating culture, fostering leadership, and creating a supportive sisterhood.
